title: Period Cramps Be Gone: Top 10 Herbal Tinctures to Try

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the uterus, making cramps less severe and uncomfortable.
Flavonoids are good for period cramps because they help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the uterus, which can ease the pain and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps.
Polyphenols are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the uterus, making them a natural remedy to alleviate menstrual discomfort.
Licorinic acid is good for period cramps because it helps to relax the muscles in the uterus, reducing pain and discomfort by acting as a natural pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory agent.
Triterpenoids are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort in the uterus, thereby providing relief from menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is made from the roots of the licorice plant, a natural remedy for many health issues. For period cramps, this tincture is beneficial because it helps relax the muscles. The root contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which blocks the production of a hormone called prostaglandin. Prostaglandin is a chemical that causes the uterus to contract, leading to cramps. By blocking its production, the tincture eases the cramping. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the lower abdomen. It also helps regulate the body's hormone balance, making it easier to manage period symptoms. Many women have found relief from period cramps by taking Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture. However, it is essential to consult a doctor before using this remedy, especially if you have underlying health conditions or take medications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis tincture that helps with period cramps, you will need valerian root, vodka or a high-proof spirit, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a coffee filter or cheesecloth. Start by drying the valerian root to remove excess moisture, then chop it into small pieces. Next, place the chopped root into the glass jar, filling it about 2/3 full. Pour vodka or a high-proof spirit over the root, making sure the root is completely covered. Secure the lid and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the tincture to extract the medicinal properties from the valerian root. After the steeping process, strain the liquid through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your valerian tincture is now ready to use. To take it, mix 20-30 drops of the tincture with water or another liquid and consume it as needed for period cramp relief.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for period cramps

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for period cramps because it helps relax muscles and reduce pain by blocking the production of certain chemicals that cause inflammation and discomfort.

Shogaol is good for period cramps because it helps to reduce pain and inflammation by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, providing relief from menstrual cramps and discomfort.
Zingerone is good for period cramps because it helps to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, which can help to ease the pain and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help ease period cramps. The tincture is made from the root of the plant, which contains a compound called gingerol. When gingerol is absorbed into the body, it blocks the production of certain chemicals that cause pain and inflammation in the uterus. This helps to reduce the severity of cramps and makes them more manageable. Ginger is also a natural anti-inflammatory, which means it reduces swelling and redness in the body, further contributing to relief from period cramps. Additionally, ginger has a calming effect on the stomach and digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea and other symptoms that often accompany period cramps. By using Zingiber officinale tincture, women may be able to find some relief from the discomfort of period cramps, allowing them to go about their daily lives with more ease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures that help with period cramps, start by gathering organic Valerian root, a clean glass jar, a saucepan, and a bottle for storing the tincture. Begin by drying the Valerian root in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying it, to prevent any moisture from spoiling the herb. Once the root is completely dry, chop it into small pieces and place them in the glass jar. Next, pour a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or glycerin over the dried Valerian root, making sure that the root is completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the liquid extract the healing properties from the Valerian root. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the storage bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts, 20-30 drops, mixed with water or another liquid, to help ease period cramps and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for period cramps

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Phenolic acids are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort in the uterus and reduce the severity of menstrual cramps.

Isoferulic acid is good for period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and cause cramping.
Lignans are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain and discomfort.
Ferulic acid is good for period cramps because it helps to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can ease the pain and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps.
Quercetin is good for period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the uterus, making it easier to manage menstrual discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tincture is made from a plant called black cohosh. For many years, people have used black cohosh to help with period cramps. The tincture is thought to work by relaxing the muscles in the uterus, which can become tight and painful during menstruation. This relaxation helps to reduce the amount of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause cramps and discomfort. Black cohosh also contains compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and ease pain. Some people find that taking Cimicifuga racemosa tincture helps to reduce the severity and frequency of period cramps, making it easier to manage symptoms and go about daily life. This tincture is often used by women who experience moderate to severe period cramps, and it can be taken alongside other natural remedies or with a doctor's advice.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for period cramps, start by gathering dried Valerian root, a glass jar with a lid, a clean glass dropper bottle, and a high-proof liquor like vodka or brandy. Begin by filling the glass jar with dried Valerian root, making sure it's fully covered with the liquor, and then seal the jar. Place the jar in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking it every day or two to help the liquor extract the active ingredients from the root. After the two to three weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the glass dropper bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture is now ready to use - take 20-30 drops of the Valerian root tincture in water or another liquid as needed to help alleviate period cramps. Note: Before using the tincture, consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications, as Valerian root may interact with certain substances.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Paeoniflorin is good for period cramps because it can help relax the muscles in the uterus and reduce inflammation, which in turn eases the pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

Polyphenols are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and relax the muscles in the uterus, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Flavonoids are good for period cramps because they help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which in turn ease the severe pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

Saponins are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort by relaxing the uterine muscles and reducing the production of prostaglandins, which cause cramping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora, also known as peony root, is a plant-based remedy that has been used for centuries to ease period cramps. The tincture of this plant contains compounds that act on the body's nervous system, helping to reduce pain and inflammation. Peony root tincture works by blocking the release of certain chemicals in the brain that send pain signals to the body, which in turn reduces the severity of cramps. This natural remedy also contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the uterus and surrounding muscles, further reducing cramping and discomfort. When taken internally, the tincture can help to relax the body and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to manage period cramps and other associated symptoms. Additionally, peony root tincture may help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to menstrual cramps, making it a potentially effective solution for women experiencing severe or frequent period pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tincture for period cramps, start by gathering dried Valerian root and high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. Fill the jar about 1/4 with dried root, and then top it with vodka, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Close the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This allows the vodka to extract the medicinal properties from the Valerian root. After the waiting period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place, and use it as needed by taking 20-30 drops in water or another liquid, up to 3 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for period cramps because they help relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, which in turn decreases the pain and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps.

Triterpenoids, also known as valerian root, help relieve period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that calm the muscles and reduce discomfort in the lower abdomen.

Iridoid glycosides are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to cramping.
Saponins are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax muscles and reduce pain in the uterus, thereby providing relief from menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus, also known as chasteberry, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual issues. The tincture made from this plant is believed to help with period cramps because it has hormone-regulating properties. It helps to balance the body's natural hormone levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can become imbalanced and cause pain and discomfort during menstruation. When these hormone levels are in balance, it can reduce the severity of period cramps. Some studies have shown that Vitex agnus-castus can also have a positive effect on mood and can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to period cramps. Additionally, it may help to regulate the menstrual cycle, making periods more regular and predictable. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Vitex agnus-castus tincture can provide relief from period cramps and make menstruation more manageable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis herbal tincture that helps with period cramps, you will need dried valerian root, high-proof vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar. First, fill a clean glass jar with one part of dried valerian root, and then fill the jar to the top with high-proof vodka or glycerin. The ratio is usually one part herb to one part solvent. Next, seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the tincture extract from the valerian root. This process is called maceration and it allows the solvent to draw out the medicinal properties from the herb. After the maceration period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be stored in a cool, dark place and taken as needed to help alleviate period cramps.

Coumarins are good for period cramps because they help to relax the muscles in the uterus and reduce inflammation, which can ease the pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

Butylphthalide may help with period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and tighten.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as Dong Quai, is a traditional herbal remedy used to ease period cramps. The tincture is made from the roots of the plant, which contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and discomfort. When taken before or during a menstrual period, Angelica sinensis tincture can help relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms. Its antispasmodic properties also help to calm the body's natural response to hormonal fluctuations, which can lead to painful period symptoms. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has been shown to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal levels, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the severity of period cramps. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Angelica sinensis tincture can help women find relief from the discomfort and pain associated with period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for period cramps, start by gathering high-quality dried Valerian root or Valerian flowers. You will also need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like glycerin or vegetable oil, and a menstruation tea or vodka as a solvent. Begin by filling the jar about a quarter with the dried Valerian root or flowers, then pour the solvent over the herbs, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the herbs' active compounds. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. To make the tincture more concentrated, you can simmer the liquid gently for a few hours or add more Valerian root and solvent to the jar and let it sit for another week. Once you have your Valerian tincture, you can mix a few drops with a carrier oil or glycerin to create a soothing salve for period cramp relief. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are pregnant.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is a natural remedy that can help ease period cramps. The main reason is that it improves blood flow, which helps to relax the muscles in the uterus. When the muscles in the uterus relax, the cramps become less severe. Ginkgo biloba also reduces inflammation, which is another cause of period cramps. The tincture works by opening up the blood vessels and allowing more blood to flow to the affected area. This helps to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with period cramps. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. This can also help to reduce the severity of period cramps. Some people also find that Ginkgo biloba tincture helps to regulate their menstrual cycle, which can lead to lighter and shorter periods.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tincture for period cramps, start by gathering dried Valerian root from a trusted supplier or drying and cleaning your own fresh roots. Combine one part of the dried root with one part of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, making sure the herb is completely covered. Next, label and date the jar, then place it in a cool, dark place and let it steep for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the herb's properties. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the tincture to smaller glass bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take 20-30 drops in water three to four times a day as needed to help alleviate period cramps.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Phenolic acids are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and cramping.
Coumarins help reduce period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the uterus and relax the muscles, reducing pain and discomfort.
Furocoumarins are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the uterus and reduce pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tincture is a natural remedy for period cramps. This herbal tincture is made from the roots of the angelica plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. It contains a compound called sesquiterpene, which helps to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the body. When you have period cramps, your uterus contracts and your muscles tighten, causing pain. The sesquiterpene in angelica archangelica tincture works to calm down these muscle contractions and relax your uterus, providing relief from cramps. It also has antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce spasms and ease pain. Some people find that taking angelica archangelica tincture helps to reduce the severity and frequency of period cramps, and can even help to regulate their menstrual cycle. It's often used in combination with other herbal remedies, like ginger or cramp bark, to provide even more relief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tincture for period cramps, start by drying the Valerian root in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying it in a dark place. Once dried, chop the root into small pieces and combine it with a neutral solvent like vodka in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is one part dried root to five parts vodka, but you can adjust this to your liking. Secure the lid and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You can now use this tincture as a natural remedy for period cramps by taking a few drops in water or tea, typically 2-5 milliliters, 2-3 times a day. Keep in mind that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're pregnant or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia officinalis, also known as peony root, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to ease period cramps. The root of the peony plant contains a compound called paeoniflorin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When taken as a tincture, paeonia officinalis can help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity of cramps and spasms. This is especially helpful for women who experience intense, debilitating cramps during their period. The tincture can also help to balance hormone levels and regulate menstrual cycles, further reducing the risk of cramps. Many women have found that paeonia officinalis tincture provides quick relief from period cramps, allowing them to go about their daily activities with more ease and comfort. Some people also report that the tincture can help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, which can be especially helpful during the premenstrual phase.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for period cramps, start by gathering a few dried Valerian root pieces and a clean glass jar with a lid. Fill the jar about 2/3 full with the dried Valerian root, then pour enough vodka or grain alcohol to cover the roots completely. The ratio is typically 1 part Valerian root to 2 parts liquor. Make sure the Valerian roots are completely submerged in the liquid. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, like a cupboard or drawer, for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, and it allows the active compounds from the Valerian root to infuse into the liquor. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Valeriana officinalis tincture is now ready to use - you can take a few drops in water or tea to help alleviate period cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture is often used to help with period cramps. This is because it contains a compound called valerenic acid, which has a calming effect on the body. When taken, it can help to relax the muscles in the uterus, reducing the severity of cramps. It also has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, common symptoms that often come with period cramps. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the lower abdomen. Some people also find that it helps to regulate their menstrual cycle and reduce the frequency of heavy periods. By taking Valeriana officinalis tincture, women may be able to manage their period cramps more effectively and improve their overall comfort during this time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Valeriana officinalis tincture that helps with period cramps, start by gathering the necessary materials: dried Valerian root, a clean glass jar, a measuring cup, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth or coffee filter. Begin by measuring out one part of dried Valerian root for every one part of solvent, so if you're using a 1 cup jar, use 1/2 cup of root and 1/2 cup of solvent. Place the root in the jar and pour the solvent over it, making sure the root is fully covered. Stir the mixture well and seal the jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion time, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use, and you can take 20-30 drops in water or tea as needed to help alleviate period cramps.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a natural and effective way to alleviate period cramps. One of the key herbs used is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle spasms and soothe the uterus. This can lead to a significant decrease in pain and discomfort. Another herb used is Zingiber officinale, or ginger, which has natural pain-relieving properties. It works by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.  Period cramps can be debilitating and affect daily activities. They can lead to fatigue, mood swings, and difficulty concentrating. Ignoring the issue can cause it to worsen over time, impacting not only physical but also mental well-being. By addressing period cramps, individuals can improve their quality of life. They can engage in activities they enjoy, maintain a healthy diet, and participate in regular exercise. This, in turn, can boost mood, energy levels, and overall resilience.  Herbal tinctures offer a safe and natural alternative to conventional pain relief medication, which can have unwanted side effects. They can be taken in small doses and combined with other herbs to create a customized remedy. By incorporating herbal tinctures into their routine, individuals can take control of their menstrual health and reduce their reliance on medication. This can lead to a more balanced and fulfilling life, where period cramps no longer dictate daily activities.
supplementary_best: A combination of Zingiber officinale (ginger), Cimicifuga racemosa (black cohosh), and Valeriana officinalis (valerian) tinctures may help relieve period cramps. Ginger tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling. Cimicifuga racemosa helps ease crampy discomfort and regulates hormonal imbalances. Valeriana officinalis promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system, which can contribute to cramp relief.  The combination works well because it targets multiple aspects of period cramps: pain reduction, hormonal balance, and relaxation. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties address the physical pain, while Cimicifuga racemosa addresses the hormonal imbalance that may contribute to cramps. Valeriana officinalis helps with the emotional and physical tension associated with period cramps. This combination may provide relief from period cramps by addressing the root causes and symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have period cramps, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One of them is Blue Cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) tincture. Blue Cohosh can stimulate the uterus, which may not be good for people with heavy periods or irregular menstrual cycles. Another herb to be careful with is Red Clover (Trifolium pratense) tincture. Red Clover contains isoflavones, which may act like estrogen in the body. Taking too much estrogen can lead to heavy bleeding, so it's best to be cautious with Red Clover if you have period cramps. Also, avoid Wild Yam (Dioscorea villosa) tincture, as it can stimulate the uterus and make period cramps worse. Finally, Black Cohosh (Actaea racemosa) tincture is not recommended for period cramps. Black Cohosh contains compounds that can stimulate the uterus and cause heavy bleeding. It's best to talk to a healthcare professional before taking any herbal tinctures if you have period cramps.
supplementary_other_remedies: Warm baths are often used to help with period cramps. The heat from the water can help relax the muscles in the uterus, which can be a major contributor to the pain. As the muscles relax, they release tension, allowing the blood vessels to expand and improve blood flow to the area. This increased blood flow can help to deliver pain-relieving chemicals, such as endorphins, to the uterus.  Castor oil packs are another natural remedy that can help with period cramps. Castor oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the uterus and surrounding tissues. When applied to the lower abdomen in the form of a warm compress, the oil can help to relax the muscles and reduce pain. The increased blood flow and reduced inflammation can also help to prepare the uterus for menstruation, making the experience less painful.  Heat therapy, such as using a heating pad or a warm compress, can also provide relief from period cramps. The heat can help to relax the muscles in the uterus and surrounding tissues, reducing pain and discomfort. Heat therapy can also help to increase blood flow to the area, delivering pain-relieving chemicals to the uterus.  Aromatherapy, specifically using essential oils like clary sage and lavender, can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can in turn help to alleviate period cramps. These oils can be inhaled through steam inhalation, added to bath water, or applied topically to the skin with a carrier oil. The relaxing effects of these oils can help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of calm, making period cramps less severe.  Finally, dietary changes, such as increasing magnesium and omega-3 intake, can also help to alleviate period cramps. Magnesium is a natural muscle relaxant, and increasing intake can help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate pain. Omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, making the experience less painful.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience period cramps often have other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. For example, many women with period cramps also have bloating and water retention due to hormonal changes. Dandelion root tea can help reduce bloating and water retention because it acts as a diuretic, encouraging the body to release excess fluids.  Women with period cramps may also experience mood swings due to changes in estrogen and progesterone levels. Passionflower tea has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate mood swings.  Some women with period cramps may also have digestive issues like constipation or diarrhea. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea, making it a good option for treating digestive issues associated with period cramps.  Women with period cramps may also experience fatigue and low energy levels due to the release of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and relax. Ashwagandha is an adaptogenic herb that can help increase energy levels and reduce fatigue by balancing the body's response to stress.  Many women with period cramps also experience headaches and migraines due to hormonal changes and prostaglandin release. Feverfew is a medicinal herb that has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches and migraines.
